rationale: Healthcare at baseline draws on EU Facility Pillar I grants and concessional IFI debt. ERA co-financing provides a politically salient layer. ECA buyer credit de-risks equipment procurement. Public counterpart equity is mandatory for IFI disbursement.

EU building-code and accessibility conditionalities unlock a higher grant share under Pillar I. Concessional debt remains the workhorse; reduced public-equity requirement reflects the increased grant coverage of the upgrade premium.

sources.ref: Ukraine Plan Pillar I conditionality framework

rationale: EU green/energy-efficiency conditionalities and medical-technology grants push the grant share to 55%+ for BBB. ERA co-financing provides political legitimacy.

sources.ref: Ukraine Plan Pillar I BBB conditionality and green standards
Green Economy Transition framework for social assets

rationale: Education at baseline draws on EU Facility Pillar I grants and concessional IFI debt. ERA co-financing provides a politically salient layer. ECA cover applies to equipment and furnishings procurement.

sources.ref: EBRD Ukraine Solidarity Package education precedents

rationale: EU building-code and energy-efficiency conditionalities unlock a higher grant share. Concessional debt absorbs the compliance cost; public equity reduced correspondingly.

sources.ref: Ukraine Plan Pillar I conditionality framework

rationale: EU green standards and digital-education conditionality unlock the maximum grant share. ERA co-financing provides political legitimacy for the BBB premium.

sources.ref: Ukraine Plan Pillar I BBB conditionality and green standards
Green Economy Transition framework for social assets

rationale: Heritage assets attract UNESCO, Creative Europe, and EU Cultural Heritage programmes on top of Facility Pillar I, pushing the grant share above social infrastructure norms. ECA cover applies to specialist conservation materials and equipment.

sources.ref: EBRD Ukraine cultural heritage rehabilitation precedents

rationale: Seismic and energy-performance standards for historic buildings attract additional heritage-specific EU funding. Specialist conservation techniques increase costs but are largely grant-covered.

sources.ref: Ukraine Plan Pillar I conditionality framework

rationale: UNESCO World Heritage and Creative Europe BBB programmes alongside EU Facility green standards push heritage grant share to maximum. Conservation premium fully grant-funded; minimal public equity residual.

sources.ref: §Culture and Heritage Sector Financing, BBB scenario
Ukraine Plan Pillar I BBB conditionality

rationale: Water and sanitation at baseline relies heavily on concessional IFI debt (EBRD/EIB/World Bank) plus EU Facility grants. ECA cover de-risks equipment imports. Public equity counterpart mandatory for IFI participation.
EU water quality and environmental standards conditionality unlocks higher grant share. Concessional debt absorbs the standards-upgrade cost; public equity reduced accordingly.

sources.ref: Ukraine Plan Pillar I conditionality framework
EBRD Ukraine water sector code-compliant precedents

rationale: EU Green Deal and circular-economy conditionality for water BBB unlocks maximum grant. Institutional debt (pension/insurer takeout) enters at BBB due to the infrastructure-bond characteristics of long-duration water assets.

sources.ref: §Water and Sanitation Sector Financing, BBB scenario
Ukraine Plan Pillar I BBB conditionality
EBRD Green Cities Ukraine — water BBB precedents

rationale: District-scale residential reconstruction at baseline. EU Housing Pillar and EBRD concessional lending dominate; small private equity wedge represents premium-segment infill and developer co-investment in mixed-tenure schemes.
First-loss sleeve introduced at code-compliant path to de-risk the energy-efficiency and seismic-standard upgrade premium. EU Housing Pillar grant share rises with green conditionality.

sources.ref: EBRD Green Cities Ukraine — residential retrofit precedents

rationale: EU Housing Pillar + EBRD Green Cities concessional unlocks the BBB premium for near-passive-house or mass-timber construction. Institutional debt enters at BBB for long-duration housing bonds; first-loss sleeve de-risks private developer participation.

sources.ref: EBRD Green Cities — BBB residential district precedents

rationale: Energy generation and grid at baseline. Senior IFI (EBRD/EIB) debt is the primary instrument; commercial debt enters only via IFI A/B loan. MIGA war-risk wrap is structurally required. First-loss sleeve (EU Facility Pillar II guarantee) de-risks the commercial tranche. ECA cover applies to equipment import.

sources.ref: EBRD Ukraine Energy Resilience financing precedents
MIGA War & Civil Disturbance — Ukraine energy portfolio

rationale: EU energy conditionality (grid modernisation, smart metering, efficiency standards) unlocks a larger grant envelope. Senior IFI share decreases as higher grant coverage reduces the financing gap. ECA cover mandatory for equipment import.

sources.ref: EBRD Ukraine energy sector code-compliant precedents

rationale: EU Green Deal grants (renewable conversion, grid flexibility) and EBRD GET concessional debt dominate the BBB stack. ECA cover mandatory. DFI equity essential for the restructured SOE equity stub. Institutional debt via mini-perm takeout for long-duration infrastructure bonds.

sources.ref: Ukraine Plan energy BBB conditionality and renewable targets
EBRD GET Ukraine energy — fuel switch precedents
MIGA War & Civil Disturbance — Ukraine energy portfolio

rationale: Revenue-generating transport (ports, airports, metro, shipyards) attracts commercial and institutional debt via A/B loan structure. Senior IFI debt anchors the stack. MIGA and ECA cover essential for the commercial tranche in Ukraine's risk environment.

sources.ref: MIGA War & Civil Disturbance — transport portfolio

rationale: EU TEN-T compliance and green port/airport standards increase grant share marginally. Private equity reduces slightly as compliance cost is partially grant-covered.

BBB green port or smart airport upgrades attract EU Connecting Europe Facility grants and EBRD GET concessional. Institutional debt tranche increases at BBB as upgraded asset generates bond-eligible long-duration cash flows.

sources.ref: Ukraine Plan Connecting Europe Facility integration

rationale: Non-revenue transport (bridges, rail) mirrors the social infrastructure stack. Primarily grants and concessional IFI debt with public-sector equity counterpart. ECA cover for equipment/materials import. No commercial or private-equity tranche viable.

EU TEN-T and safety standards conditionality increases grant share. Concessional debt absorbs the standard-upgrade cost; public equity reduced correspondingly. Increased ECA tranche for higher-specification equipment.

EU Connecting Europe Facility and TEN-T grants cover the BBB premium (rail electrification, ETCS signalling, bridge resilience standards). Concessional tranche maintained; public equity shrinks as grant expansion absorbs the BBB uplift.

Industrial assets (steel, coke, agri-processing) are commercially driven — private equity is the dominant tranche. IFI senior debt and DFI equity play a catalytic role. Large ECA tranche for capital equipment. MIGA wrap essential for commercial participation.

sources.ref: EBRD Ukraine industrial recovery — Metinvest and agri-business precedents

rationale: EU environmental standards (Best Available Techniques for steel/coke) attract limited environmental grant supplement. Private equity reduces as compliance cost is partially covered. Institutional debt enters for longer-duration component.

EU Innovation Fund and green hydrogen grants increase the grant share at BBB for low-carbon industrial transitions. Private equity shrinks as green conditionality requires higher grant/concessional; institutional debt grows to fill gap with long-duration infrastructure characteristics.

sources.ref: EBRD Ukraine industrial recovery — green transition precedents

rationale: Government buildings exclusively grant/concessional-financed. High grant share reflects rule-of-law and governance conditionality in EU Facility Pillar I. No commercial or private tranche applicable.

sources.ref: Ukraine Plan governance and rule-of-law milestones

rationale: EU building-code conditionality (energy performance, accessibility) increases grant share. Concessional tranche reduced as the grant envelope absorbs the compliance cost uplift.

sources.ref: Ukraine Plan governance reform and public building standards

rationale: Near-passive or BREEAM Excellent standard qualifies for EU Green New Deal grant support in addition to standard governance grants. Maximum grant concentration — concessional debt covers only the residual financing gap.

sources.ref: Ukraine Plan BBB public buildings conditionality
